'L'exploitant justifie, dans un délai d'un mois, que...' is the inspector's injunction, not a future commitment made by the exploitant — R02 requires an exploitant-initiated promise. Reclassified as a factual/procedural NC (R14_NEUTRE_NC).

'Dans un délais de 3 mois, l'exploitant s'assure de corriger ces non-conformités.'

'L'exploitant a déclaré que les 3 chaudières ne peuvent techniquement pas fonctionner ensemble' is a present-tense factual/technical claim, not an unverifiable future commitment — matches the taxonomy's explicit counter-example for R02 misuse.

error_patterns: One dominant systematic pattern accounts for 47 of 52 corrections: Hunter never populated the `modifiers` array (0/130 records had a non-empty modifiers field). Nine records put m_MENACE inside `mechanisms` instead — a STRICT code-validity violation, corrected by relocation. The other 29 modifier corrections are net-new: constats_body frequently contains an explicit numeric corrective deadline ('dans un délai de X jours/mois/an', 'sous X mois') and/or a conditional threat of mise en demeure ('s'expose à des suites administratives de type mise en demeure', 'passé ce délai, l'inspection proposera...') that Hunter's reasoning sometimes even paraphrases in prose ('Menace MED', 'délai 2 mois') without setting the corresponding modifier code. This confirms and extends the batch-generic pattern flagged for audit: m_DELAI was undershot everywhere (0 uses on 38 eligible records) rather than overshot. Three records (pfa-logistic f04/f07/f08) had a formal demand-with-delay in constats_body but were staged S01_CONSTAT instead of S02_INJONCTION, a secondary consequence of the same missed-delay blind spot. Two records showed the specific R02 over-attribution failure mode named in the audit brief: 'délai...l'exploitant justifie/précise' phrasing was read as an exploitant promise when it was actually the inspector's injunction (picoty-aquitaine ...f12) or a present-tense factual/technical claim rather than a future commitment (piscine-du-pinsan ...f12) — both reclassified R02→R14. No instances of the other flagged pattern (R10_TENSION misused for unfulfilled-promise cases) were found — R10 was never used in this batch. No false M08, no missed multi-label, no over-aggressive M17, and no gravity/trajectory errors were found in the 20-record high-confidence spot-check or in the ~45 records read directly against local/corpus-all during this audit.
verdict: Hunter's substantive classification (domains, mechanisms, gravity, trajectory, dynamic, stage) is accurate and well-reasoned across the batch — the axis-1/axis-2 multi-labeling, G3-before-G2 physical-NC heuristic, and T-axis history tracking are all applied correctly, including in chained follow-up fiches (piscine-du-pinsan, polyprocess, picoty) that require reconciling prior-inspection context with current-visit resolution. The one real defect is structural and total: the `modifiers` axis was not used at all, which is a systematic omission rather than scattered misjudgment — every fix in this batch traces back to either relocating a misplaced m_MENACE or adding a missing m_DELAI/m_MENACE that the constats_body text supports unambiguously. After correction, modifiers are populated on 39/130 records (30%), consistent with how often this corpus states an explicit corrective deadline or a conditional MED threat. Batch is sound post-correction.
